fix: proper system detection for all four default platforms

function that uses Rust's cfg attributes to properly detect all four
supported platforms at compile time:
- x86_64-linux
- aarch64-linux
- x86_64-darwin
- aarch64-darwin

commit.message:
fix: apply USER_UPDATED event so profile edits (phone, name, address) take effect immediately

The onAuthStateChange guard was skipping all events for the same user ID
to prevent TOKEN_REFRESHED from causing a fetchOrders loop. This also
blocked USER_UPDATED, so profile changes saved to user_metadata were
never reflected in the React state until a full page reload.

cleanup: remove debug logs after resolving Ori AI chat auth issue

Root cause: Supabase gateway's built-in JWT verification rejects ES256 tokens
(used by newer Supabase projects) before the function code runs. Fix: deploy
with --no-verify-jwt so our function handles auth via userSupabase.auth.getUser()
using the official anon-key + Authorization header pattern.

commit.message:
Fix paneldev: context switch fs routing, error code preservation, symlinked git state

Three fixes that unblock the paneldev skill's create-edit-launch workflow:

1. Cross-context fs routing: PanelView.handleCrossContextNavigation now calls
   panelLifecycle.updatePanelContext() so the fsService mapping stays current
   after a context switch. Previously eval's RPC-backed fs routed to the
   pre-switch context folder.

2. Error code preservation across RPC: ServiceError, RpcServer wire format,
   and bridge client now propagate error codes (ENOENT, etc.) so isomorphic-git
   can distinguish missing files from real errors.

3. Context folders now include .git: setupContextGit() copies mutable state
   (HEAD, index, refs, config, info) per-context and symlinks the immutable
   object store, so commitAndPush works as a proper fast-forward push against
   existing repo history. Also adds isSymbolicLink to stat serialization for
   isomorphic-git compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  (Vercel NODE_ENV=production skips devDeps during npm install)

  (eliminates need for --legacy-peer-deps)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

📦NEW: stream AI responses progressively to the DOM (#26)

* feat: stream AI responses progressively to the DOM

Both OpenAI and Anthropic providers now use SSE streaming so text
appears character-by-character instead of waiting for the full
response. Shared buffered SSE line-parsing logic is extracted into
sseParser.ts to avoid duplication between providers.

- sseParser.ts: shared parseSSEStream helper (buffered readline)
- openai.ts / anthropic.ts: add stream:true, parse SSE body,
  call onChunk for each text delta
- ai.ts / content.ts: thread onChunk callback through so the DOM
  is updated incrementally on every chunk
- jest.setup.ts: expose TextEncoder/TextDecoder/ReadableStream for
  SSE stream tests in jsdom environment

Las páginas de destinos y pro-tips recientes se crearon sin las etiquetas

interpretara todo el HTML como CSS (página en blanco). También faltaba
la regla .nav-links a {} del navbar.

fix: switch incremental analysis to delta mode, fix Ollama timeout

- Delta prompt: ask model for only new/changed characters per chapter,
  merge server-side — eliminates character dropping on large casts (90+)
- Previous character state sent as compact name/status/location list
  instead of full JSON, dramatically reducing prompt token usage
- Fix UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT: add 10-minute AbortController to Ollama
  fetch instead of relying on undici's default 300s headers timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update get_service_active_state docstring for LE 4.0.x reality

The original docstring described systemd ActiveState values and referenced
loopedge-analytics2 as an example. On LE 4.0.x, only the ssh service is
controllable via the API and its status response uses {"status": "started"}
not {"ActiveState": "active"}. Updated the docstring to document both
response formats, the limitation to canStartStop services, and the correct
example service name.

commit.message:
Config-store v2, 9 native LLM backends, discord/signal frontends

Upgrade config-store into the single source of truth for all non-secret
configuration. Eliminate ~53 raw env::var() calls across 30+ crates.
Setup writes directly to vault and config-store — no more runtime.env.

Config-store v2 adds: SQLite-backed scoped KV with in-memory cache,
component access policies mirroring vault, env var fallback chain
(cache → DB → legacy alias → canonical HARMONIA_{SCOPE}_{KEY} → default),
legacy env var seeding on first init, and 22 unit tests.

Add 9 native LLM backend crates (openai, anthropic, xai, groq, alibaba,
google-ai-studio, google-vertex, amazon-bedrock) with shared
provider-protocol for model pool, metrics, and FFI. Models stay as Rust
constants in provider-protocol — not in config-store.

Add discord and signal frontend crates. Restructure setup wizard into
required (workspace, SBCL, vault/config-store, LLM provider) and optional
(frontends, tools, git, S3, evolution) sections. Migrate 7 Lisp files
from sb-ext:posix-getenv to config-get-for/config-get-or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: cache dark mode in AsyncStorage for instant theme on launch

ThemeContext was starting with isDarkMode=false and loading from
Firestore async — causing tab bar (and all UI) to flash white before
the preference loaded. Now reads from AsyncStorage first (near-instant)
then syncs with Firestore in background.

commit.message:
Add auto-restore for Pro users to prevent onboarding data loss

Pro users on new devices/browsers now auto-restore from Firestore
instead of going through onboarding. This prevents empty accounts
from being created and synced, which was overwriting real data.

Flow:
- Pro user opens new browser → auto-restore from Firestore
- If Firestore has data → restore it, skip onboarding
- If Firestore empty → proceed to onboarding (new user)

Fix radial menu always opening at stale position

The DOM-reuse optimization skipped updating left/top on item elements
when the item count matched, causing the menu to always render at the
position of its first open. Now left/top are always updated, and
closeRadialMenu clears items/SVG so the entry animation replays fresh.

fix(ansm): show blocked products from catalog when ANSM import table is empty

The blocked products list was showing "Aucun produit bloque" despite 39 products
being flagged as is_ansm_blocked=true in the products table. The list only queried
ansm_blocked_products (populated by CSV import), which was empty. Now falls back
to displaying blocked products from the products table when no import data exists.

commit.message:
Add Qwen3.5 module with GDN hybrid architecture and VL support

- Custom OpenVINO export for Qwen3.5's GDN (Gated DeltaNet) hybrid architecture
  with traceable RecurrentAttentionCell converted to OV Loop nodes
- Text-only and vision-language inference sharing the same decoder IR
  (inputs_embeds entry point, no IR surgery needed)
- Vision encoder export with spatial merge (576→144 tokens)
- Embedding table extracted as numpy for Python-side lookup
- mRoPE position IDs with 3D spatial coordinates for VL
- Stateful KV/conv/recurrent cache for efficient autoregressive decoding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix three-dot menu toggle: prevent mousedown/click race condition

The document-level mousedown handler was closing the context menu
before the button's click handler could check whether to toggle it
closed. By the time onClick fired, contextMenu was already null,
so the toggle check failed and the menu re-opened immediately.

Fix: add data-context-trigger attribute to three-dot buttons and
check for it in the document handler — if the click is on a trigger
button, skip the outside-click close and let onClick handle the
toggle. Also tighten the toggle check to match both id AND type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix session cycling: compare by filename not ObjectIdentifier

ObjectIdentifier is pointer-based — on every session reopen,
ImageCaptureCore allocates brand-new ICCameraFile objects, so all OIDs
change and every file appears 'new', causing every photo to be
re-downloaded each cycle.

Fix: switch priorSessionFileIDs (Set<ObjectIdentifier>) to
priorSessionFileNames (Set<String>) so we compare by the stable
filename (e.g. "DSC00042.JPG") that persists across session reopens.

Also add a visible session open counter + file count to the connection
status label so cycling can be confirmed in the UI without Xcode:
"Connected: Alpha 7 IV [open #3, 47 files]"

commit.message:
Fix Hunyuan3D handler: fix load_model early return and remove silent catch

The early return checked both pipelines with 'and', so if only one
failed to load, subsequent load_model() calls would skip reloading.
Also remove try/catch on cold start so pipeline load failures are
visible instead of silently leaving pipelines as None.
